examples.title: Algorithm: Genesis Block Creation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateGenesisBlock
INPUT: timestamp, message
OUTPUT: genesisBlock

BEGIN
 genesisBlock.height = 0
 genesisBlock.previousHash = NULL
 genesisBlock.timestamp = timestamp
 genesisBlock.message = message
 genesisBlock.coinbaseReward = 50 BTC (unspendable)
 RETURN genesisBlock
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the creation of a genesis block, including setting its height, previous hash, timestamp, and coinbase reward.

examples.title: Python: Genesis Block Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of a genesis block
examples.code:
import hashlib
import time

class GenesisBlock:
 def __init__(self, timestamp, message):
 self.height = 0
 self.previousHash = '0' * 64
 self.timestamp = timestamp
 self.message = message
 self.coinbaseReward = 50 # unspendable

 def calculateHash(self):
 data = str(self.height) + self.previousHash + str(self.timestamp) + self.message
 return hashlib.sha256(data.encode()).hexdigest()

# Example usage
timestamp = int(time.time())
message = 'The Times 03/Jan/2009 Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks'
genesisBlock = GenesisBlock(timestamp, message)
print(genesisBlock.calculateHash())
examples.explanation: This Python implementation defines a GenesisBlock class with attributes for height, previous hash, timestamp, and coinbase reward. The calculateHash method computes the hash of the genesis block.

examples.title: C: Genesis Block Implementation
examples.description: C implementation of a genesis block
examples.explanation: This C implementation defines a GenesisBlock struct and functions to create and calculate the hash of a genesis block.
